Select a stored grade fit from a solved grade path

Source: R/grade.R
gp_select_grade.Rd

gp_select_grade() returns the pre-solved gp_grade_fit at a chosen penalty from a gp_grade_path. It never runs a solver: it indexes into the path's stored fits, so it is the instant way to read off one assignment after gp_grade_path() has done the work. If lambda is absent from the solved grid, it errors and reports the available grid values.

Usage

gp_select_grade(path, lambda = 0.25)

Arguments

path

A gp_grade_path object; the solved frontier to select from.

lambda

Numeric scalar; the penalty to select, which must be present in path$lambda_grid. Defaults to the KRW baseline 0.25.

Value

The stored gp_grade_fit at lambda (a list of class c("gp_grade_fit", "list")), exactly as solved on the path – no re-solve. See gp_grade() for the slot-level description of a gp_grade_fit.

See also

gp_grade_path(), gp_grade(), krw_report_card(), gp_pairwise()

Other gradepath-grade: gp_grade(), gp_grade_path(), gp_preview()

Examples

# Solve-free: pull the baseline-penalty fit from the bundled tiny fit's path.
fit <- readRDS(system.file("extdata/examples/tiny_fit.rds", package = "gradepath"))
gp_select_grade(fit$grade_path, lambda = 0.25)
#> +---------------------------------------------+
#> | gp_grade_fit  .  2 grades  .  lambda = 0.25 |
#> +---------------------------------------------+
#> | units = 24   status = optimal               |
#> | grade 1: 21                                 |
#> | grade 2: 3                                  |
#> +---------------------------------------------+
#> i  assignment: x$assignment   summary(x) for backend/channel details

# The pipeline already stored this selection; it is the same object.
identical(gp_select_grade(fit$grade_path, lambda = 0.25), fit$selected_grade)
#> [1] TRUE
